# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "1.1"

plugins:
  - name: cors
    global: true
    config:
      origins:
        - "https://app1.example.com"
        - "https://app2.example.com"
        - "http://localhost:3000"
      methods:
        - "GET"
        - "POST"
        - "PUT"
        - "DELETE"
        - "OPTIONS"
      headers:
        - "Content-Type"
        - "Authorization"
        - "X-Requested-With"
        - "Accept"
      exposed_headers:
        - "X-Custom-Response-Header"
      credentials: true
      max_age: 3600

services:
  - name: my-api-service
    host: my-api.internal
    port: 80
    plugins:
      - name: jwt
        config:
          uri_param_names:
            - "jwt"
          claims_to_verify:
            - "exp"
            - "nbf"
            - "iat"
          key_claim_name: "iss" # Example: use 'iss' claim as the key for consumer lookup
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 100
          policy: "local"
          limit_by: "consumer"

routes:
  - name: my-api-route
    paths:
      - /api/v1/data
    service: my-api-service
    plugins:
      - name: rate-limiting
        config:
          minute: 5
          policy: "local"
          limit_by: "ip"
      - name: cors
        config:
          origins:
            - "https://special-app.example.com" # Override global CORS for this specific route
          methods:
            - "GET"
            - "OPTIONS"
          headers:
            - "Content-Type"
            - "Authorization"
          credentials: true
          max_age: 600
